subtopics.title: What's in the Box
subtopics.content:
#### Player-Count Audit (from rulebook)
- **Factory displays** — The setup changes by player count: 5 (2 players), 7 (3 players), 9 (4 players).
- **Factory fill amount** — Each active Factory display gets exactly 4 tiles.
- **Tiles in bag** — Always 100 tiles total (20 of each color), regardless of player count.
- **Unused components** — Return unused Factory displays, boards, and score markers to the box.

#### Core Components
- **Tiles** — 100 total tiles.
- **Color split** — 20 each in 5 colors.
- **Bag** — 1 bag for random draws.
- **Starting player marker** — 1 marker.

#### Player Components
- **Player boards** — 1 per player.
- **Scoring markers** — 1 per player.
- **Board zones** — Pattern lines, wall, floor line, score track on each board.

#### Setup Counts by Player Count
--- 2 Players ---
- **Factory displays** — 5 active displays.
- **Tiles placed to factories at round start** — 20 tiles total (5 × 4).

--- 3 Players ---
- **Factory displays** — 7 active displays.
- **Tiles placed to factories at round start** — 28 tiles total (7 × 4).

--- 4 Players ---
- **Factory displays** — 9 active displays.
- **Tiles placed to factories at round start** — 36 tiles total (9 × 4).

subtopics.title: Board Layout
subtopics.content:
#### Setup Sequence
1. **Boards** — Give each player 1 board on the same side (colored wall for standard play).
2. **Score markers** — Put each marker on 0.
3. **Factories** — Place displays in a circle around center.
4. **Bag** — Put all 100 tiles in the bag.
5. **Starting marker** — Give it to the player who most recently visited Portugal.
6. **Factory fill** — Draw exactly 4 tiles onto each active Factory display.

#### Player-Count Layout Values
--- 2 Players ---
- **Factories in circle** — 5.
- **Initial factory tiles** — 20 total.

--- 3 Players ---
- **Factories in circle** — 7.
- **Initial factory tiles** — 28 total.

--- 4 Players ---
- **Factories in circle** — 9.
- **Initial factory tiles** — 36 total.

#### Table Zones
- **Center area** — Starts empty; receives leftovers from factories during drafting.
- **Box lid area** — Temporary storage for discarded tiles and overflow beyond floor line.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
#### What Each Player Gets
- **Board** — 1 player board.
- **Marker** — 1 scoring marker on 0.

#### What Starts Empty
- **Pattern lines** — All empty.
- **Wall spaces** — All empty.
- **Floor line** — Empty.

#### What to Check Before Turn 1
- **Line capacities** — Rows hold 1, 2, 3, 4, and 5 tiles.
- **Placement direction** — Pattern lines fill right to left.
- **Penalty zone** — Floor line applies end-of-round negative points.

subtopics.title: Starting the Game
subtopics.content:
#### Start-of-Round Procedure
1. **Marker to center** — Current starting-player holder places the marker in the center.
2. **First turn** — That player takes the first draft.
3. **Turn order** — Continue clockwise.

#### First Center Draft Rule
- **Whoever drafts from center first** — Takes the starting player marker.
- **Marker placement** — Put it in the leftmost free floor-line space.
- **Effect this round** — It counts as a floor-line penalty tile.
- **Effect next round** — That player becomes starting player.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
#### Round Structure (in order)
1. **Factory Offer** — Draft tiles until all factories and center are empty.
2. **Wall-tiling** — Move from completed pattern lines to wall and score.
3. **Prepare Next Round** — Refill factories and continue unless end trigger occurred.

#### 1) Factory Offer
- **Turn action** — Draft exactly one color from one source (one factory or center).
- **Factory draft** — Move non-picked tiles from that factory to center.
- **Center draft** — If first center drafter, take starting marker to floor line.
- **Placement** — Put drafted tiles into one legal pattern line; overflow goes to floor line.

#### 2) Wall-tiling
- **Completed lines only** — Move the rightmost tile of each completed line to matching wall space.
- **Immediate scoring** — Score each moved tile as placed.
- **Leftover cleanup** — Discard remaining tiles from completed lines to box lid.
- **Incomplete lines** — Stay on board for next round.
- **Floor penalties** — Apply negatives, then clear floor line to lid (except starting marker stays with player).

#### 3) Prepare Next Round
- **Refill rule** — Fill each active Factory display with 4 tiles.
- **Bag empty rule** — Refill bag from box lid, then continue filling factories.
- **Rare shortage rule** — If bag and lid both empty, begin round even with partially filled factories.

#### Active Factory Displays by Player Count
--- 2 Players ---
- **Active displays** — 5.

--- 3 Players ---
- **Active displays** — 7.

--- 4 Players ---
- **Active displays** — 9.

subtopics.title: Actions & Options
subtopics.content:
#### Draft Action (your turn)
- **Option A: Factory draft** — Take all tiles of one color from one Factory display.
- **Factory remainder** — Move all non-picked tiles from that display to center.
- **Option B: Center draft** — Take all tiles of one color from center.
- **Center-first bonus/cost** — First player to draft from center takes starting marker to floor line.

#### Placement Constraints
- **One destination line** — Place drafted tiles in one pattern line only.
- **Line color lock** — A line can hold only one color.
- **Row legality** — You cannot stage a color in a row that already has that color on the wall.
- **Overflow handling** — Any unplaceable tiles go to floor line from left to right.
- **Floor full rule** — Additional overflow goes to box lid.

#### Wall-tiling Scoring Procedure
1. **Move tile** — From each completed pattern line, move rightmost tile to matching wall space.
2. **Check adjacency** — If no orthogonal neighbors, score 1.
3. **Horizontal score** — If connected horizontally, score size of horizontal group including new tile.
4. **Vertical score** — If connected vertically, score size of vertical group including new tile.
5. **Dual connection** — If both exist, score both values.

#### Floor-Line Penalty Procedure
- **Penalty source** — Each occupied floor slot applies its printed negative value.
- **Minimum score** — Score cannot drop below 0.
- **Cleanup** — Move floor tiles to lid after penalties.
- **Starting marker exception** — Counts for penalty, then stays in front of player.

subtopics.title: Special Mechanics
subtopics.content:
#### Core Tension: Draft Value vs Penalty Risk
- **Big grabs** — Taking many tiles can complete lines faster.
- **Penalty risk** — Extra tiles can force heavy floor-line losses.

#### Pattern-Line Conversion Rule
- **Only one tile advances** — Completing a line moves only its rightmost tile to the wall.
- **Efficiency cost** — Remaining tiles from that completed line are discarded to lid.

#### Initiative Economy
- **Center access** — Can secure next-round first pick.
- **Immediate tax** — Starting marker adds floor-line penalty this round.

#### Carryover Planning
- **Incomplete lines persist** — Unfinished pattern lines remain between rounds.
- **Commitment pressure** — Persistent lines lock color options and shape future drafts.

#### Tile Recycling Loop
- **Discard route** — Used/overflow tiles go to box lid.
- **Bag refresh** — Lid refills bag only when bag empties during refill.

#### Variant Mode (Gray Side)
- **Placement freedom** — Wall placement can be in any space of the row.
- **No repeats** — A color cannot repeat in any row or column.
- **No valid space case** — If a completed line has no legal wall space, all tiles from that line go immediately to floor line.

subtopics.title: Endgame & Scoring
subtopics.content:
#### End Trigger
- **Trigger condition** — End after the Wall-tiling phase where at least one player completes a horizontal line of 5 tiles.
- **Timing** — Finish that Wall-tiling phase, then do final bonuses.

#### Ongoing Scoring
- **Wall placement points** — Score each moved wall tile immediately by adjacency rules.
- **Floor penalties** — Apply at end of every Wall-tiling phase.
- **Score floor** — Score never drops below 0.

#### Final Bonus Scoring
- **Complete horizontal line** — +2 points each.
- **Complete vertical line** — +7 points each.
- **Complete color set (5 of one color on wall)** — +10 points each.

#### Winner and Tie-break
- **Winner** — Highest total points.
- **Tie-breaker 1** — More complete horizontal lines wins.
- **Tie-breaker 2** — If still tied, tied players share victory.

subtopics.title: Opening Priorities
subtopics.content:
#### First 2 Rounds Checklist
- **Avoid floor losses** — Prefer a smaller legal draft over a large penalty-heavy draft.
- **Complete short rows early** — Push row 1 and row 2 for quick safe scoring starts.
- **Protect row legality** — Before drafting a color, confirm that row does not already contain that color on wall.

#### Position for Better Scoring
- **Build adjacency** — Prioritize placements that touch existing tiles horizontally or vertically.
- **Avoid isolated placements** — Isolated tiles cap at 1 point and slow scoring pace.

#### Manage initiative intentionally
- **Take starting marker only with plan** — Do it when first pick next round completes a key row or blocks an opponent.
- **Skip marker when weak** — If no strong first-pick target exists, avoid the penalty tax.

subtopics.title: Common Mistakes
subtopics.content:
#### High-Impact Mistakes
- **Overdrafting one factory** — Excess tiles spill to floor line and erase gained points.
- **Missing row color lockout** — Drafted color has no legal row because that wall row already has that color.
- **Overvaluing any completion** — A completion scoring 1 is often worse than delaying for adjacency value.
- **Auto-taking starting marker** — Initiative is not always worth guaranteed penalty.
- **Ignoring end timing** — Opponents may trigger game end before you finish high-bonus lines.
- **Committing long lines too early** — You lock rows and flexibility before supply confirms the color will appear.

subtopics.title: Key Decisions
subtopics.content:
#### Decision 1: Initiative vs Immediate Points
- **Take marker now** — Gain likely first draft next round.
- **Decline marker now** — Avoid floor penalty and protect current-round score.

#### Decision 2: Self-Value vs Denial
- **Self-value pick** — Best immediate fit for your board.
- **Denial pick** — Slightly weaker for you, but breaks opponent's near-complete row.

#### Decision 3: Accelerate End vs Extend Game
- **Accelerate** — Push a 5-tile horizontal completion when your bonus structure is ahead.
- **Extend** — Delay trigger if you still need verticals/color sets to overtake.

metadata.notes: Generated from provided Azul rulebook excerpt; counts and phase/scoring details align with that source text.
